title: Founder note on undocumented deal workflows
summary: Deal teams keep rebuilding the same operating cadence in spreadsheets, chats, and memory. The repeatable work is not the model itself, but the handoff rules, diligence follow-ups, and status rituals around it.

title: Repeated prospect objection: another workflow tool
summary: Prospects worry that new systems create another destination. The strongest response has been to frame Arvya as a way to codify existing work into a lightweight operating system, not replace every tool.

title: Customer pattern: diligence context gets lost after close
summary: Operators described a recurring gap between diligence and post-close execution. Decisions, risks, and owner context are captured during the deal but rarely survive in a form the operating team can use.

title: Product update: reusable workflow templates
summary: The product team shipped a first pass at reusable workflow templates for recurring deal motions. The update is intended to make repeated work easier to inspect, improve, and assign.

title: Strategy memo: credible automation starts with process memory
summary: The memo argues that automation in deal environments fails when it begins with generic agents instead of process memory. The useful wedge is documenting how a specific firm actually makes decisions.

title: Competitor theme: dashboards without accountability
summary: Several competitor tools emphasize dashboards. Internal discussion noted that dashboards help visibility, but they do not by themselves create clear ownership or repeatable follow-through.
